req.title: Biller registration (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall register a biller only after a 24-hour hold; withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-073 mandates a 24-hour hold on newly registered billers and deferred withdrawals. The test suite covers: (1) a positive EP case confirming a valid biller lands in 'hold' state with HTTP 201; (2) a negative DT case confirming a duplicate biller returns HTTP 409; (3) a boundary BVA case confirming registration with the maximum-length biller name is accepted; (4) a negative NEG case confirming an unauthenticated request is rejected with HTTP 401; (5) a state-transition STC case — corrected from 'negative' to 'positive' kind because STC heuristic models valid state transitions (the system correctly deferring a withdrawal is the expected, specified behaviour, not an error path) — confirming that a withdrawal attempted within 24 hours of registration is deferred. The violation in test[4] (negative kind paired with STC heuristic) is fixed by changing kind to 'positive': the deferred-withdrawal behaviour is the required outcome specified in R-FIN-073, making it a positive state-transition scenario.

tests.name: test_register_biller_valid_lands_in_hold_state
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT available via <auth_fixture>)
Biller name does not already exist for this customer
Customer account is in good standing
actions: Assert hold_until is approximately NOW + 24 hours
expected: Response JSON field 'hold_until' is within [23h55m, 24h05m] from request time
trace: R-FIN-073: biller registration places biller in hold state
R-FIN-073: 24-hour hold enforced on new billers
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_register_biller_valid_lands_in_hold_state(auth_headers, api_client, unique_biller_payload):
    before = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    resp = api_client.post("/billers", json=unique_biller_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    after = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()

    assert body["status"] == "hold", f"Expected 'hold', got {body.get('status')}"

    hold_until = datetime.fromisoformat(body["hold_until"])
    lower = before + timedelta(hours=23, minutes=55)
    upper = after + timedelta(hours=24, minutes=5)
    assert lower <= hold_until <= upper, (
        f"hold_until {hold_until} not within expected 24-hour window [{lower}, {upper}]"
    )

tests.name: test_register_duplicate_biller_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT available via <auth_fixture>)
Biller with the same account number already registered for this customer (pre-seeded via <seed_biller_fixture>)
actions: POST /billers with payload whose account number matches existing biller
Assert error code in response body indicates duplicate biller
trace: R-FIN-073 error pathway: 409 if duplicate biller
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_register_duplicate_biller_returns_409(auth_headers, api_client, seeded_biller_payload):
    # seeded_biller_payload fixture pre-registers the biller and returns same payload
    resp = api_client.post("/billers", json=seeded_biller_payload, headers=auth_headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 409, f"Expected 409, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "DUPLICATE_BILLER", (
        f"Unexpected error_code: {body.get('error_code')}"
    )

    # Confirm only one biller record exists
    list_resp = api_client.get("/billers", headers=auth_headers)
    assert list_resp.status_code == 200
    billers = [b for b in list_resp.json()["billers"]
               if b["account_number"] == seeded_biller_payload["account_number"]]
    assert len(billers) == 1, f"Expected 1 record, found {len(billers)}"

tests.name: test_register_biller_max_length_name_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT available via <auth_fixture>)
API schema defines maximum biller name length of 100 characters
No existing biller with the same account number
actions: Build payload with biller_name of exactly 100 characters
expected: Biller record created with full 100-character name preserved
trace: R-FIN-073: valid biller registration accepted at upper boundary of name length
BVA on biller_name field maximum boundary (100 chars)
tests.executable:
import pytest

MAX_NAME_LEN = 100

def test_register_biller_max_length_name_accepted(auth_headers, api_client, unique_account_number):
    max_name = "B" * MAX_NAME_LEN
    payload = {
        "biller_name": max_name,
        "account_number": unique_account_number,
        "biller_code": "TST001"
    }

    resp = api_client.post("/billers", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "hold"
    assert body["biller_name"] == max_name, "Biller name not preserved at max length"

tests.name: test_register_biller_unauthenticated_returns_401
preconditions: No authentication token is provided in the request
actions: POST /billers with valid payload but no Authorization header
Assert no biller record is created
trace: R-FIN-073 precondition: customer must be authenticated
R-FIN-073 error pathway: unauthorised access rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_register_biller_unauthenticated_returns_401(api_client, unique_biller_payload):
    resp = api_client.post("/billers", json=unique_biller_payload)  # no auth headers

    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "UNAUTHORIZED", (
        f"Unexpected error_code: {body.get('error_code')}"
    )

tests.name: test_withdrawal_to_new_biller_within_hold_period_is_deferred
preconditions: Customer is authenticated (valid JWT available via <auth_fixture>)
Biller was just registered and is in 'hold' state (pre-seeded via <new_biller_in_hold_fixture>)
actions: Assert HTTP 202 with deferred status OR HTTP 403 indicating withdrawal blocked during hold
Assert withdrawal is not immediately executed
Assert withdrawal record reflects deferred or pending state
expected: HTTP 202 Accepted with response body status 'deferred' or 'pending', OR HTTP 403 with error_code 'BILLER_IN_HOLD'
No funds debited from account immediately
Withdrawal record reflects deferred/pending state confirming the system correctly enforces the 24-hour hold transition
trace: R-FIN-073: withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred during 24-hour hold
STC: state transition from 'registered' to 'hold' correctly gates withdrawal execution — deferred outcome is the specified positive behaviour
